message:
fix(doctor): detect and recreate missing FTS search table (#17)

The doctor command now checks if the fts_messages FTS5 virtual table
exists. If missing, running `cass doctor --fix` will recreate it and
repopulate it from the messages table.

This fixes the "no such table: fts_messages" error that can occur when
the FTS table is dropped or corrupted after the database reaches the
final schema version.

Changes:
- Add fts_table check in run_doctor() after database check succeeds
- If FTS table missing and --fix is set, recreate and populate it
- If FTS table missing and --fix not set, mark as failure with hint

Fixes cass_memory_system#17

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
